name: IDB mock via vi.hoisted()
description: vi.mock() hoists before variable declarations. For IDB mocks that need closure over variables, use vi.hoisted() which properly handles the hoisting order.

content: Stub check: every test must fail against a trivial stub. If a function returning [] / null / true / new Set() would pass the test, the test is too weak. Encode contracts, not implementations.
At least 1 adversarial case per test file: empty input, max size, malformed data, concurrent access. Names follow spec: 'rejects negative quantities' not 'test_3'.
Coverage thresholds: statements 80%, branches 70%, functions 80%, lines 80%. Configured in vitest.config.ts with v8 provider.
UI tests assert user-visible outcomes only. Never DOM structure, CSS classes, or component internals. No magic literals — assert relationships, not hardcoded values.
